title: Passiflora macfadyenii
taxonomy: Passiflora macfadyenii is classified within the kingdom Plantae, with its more specific classification being Passifloraceae family and Passifloroideae subfamily. This plant belongs to the genus Passiflora and is further classified within the species P. macfadyenii. The full classification of Passiflora macfadyenii is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Malpighiales, family Passifloraceae, subfamily Passifloroideae, tribe Passifloreae, genus Passiflora, species P. macfadyenii. Its classification reflects its close relationship with other species within the Passiflora genus. Understanding the classification of Passiflora macfadyenii is essential for botanical research and identification.

intro: Passiflora macfadyenii, also known as the yellow granadilla or banana passionflower, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Passiflora, characterized by its unique and intricate flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its calming and sedative effects, with reported health benefits including relief from anxiety and insomnia, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Bioactive constituents of P. macfadyenii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, P. macfadyenii is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in the production of infusions, capsules, and ointments.
